LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF KARIM ISAAC'S MOTION TO ENFORCE A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS DENIED. C C P SECTION 664.6 REQUIRES THAT AN AGREEMENT BE SIGNED BY THE PARTIES PERSONALLY IN ORDER TO OBTAIN ENFORCEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 664.6. AN ATTORNEY'S SIGNATURE ON BEHALF OF A CLIENT IS INSUFFICIENT FOR THIS REMEDY. LEVY VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 10 CAL. 4TH 578. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DOCUMENTS IS DENIED, MATTER TO BE RENOTICED IN DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

ATTY. B. CLYDE HUTCHINSON OF LOMBARDI, LOPER & CONANT, TEL. NO. (510) 433-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PLAINTIFF KARIM ISAAC'S Renewed Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement; Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Documents- MOTION TO ENFORCE A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED AS TO GYM MEMBERSHIP. MOTION TO COMPEL IS OFF CALENDAR AND TO BE NOTICED IN DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR#: 8189 =301/PHA/AL

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2007 Continued To Jan-08-08 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice And Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement, Pltt'S Motion To Compel Documents
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John Furstenthal, Esq. of Hildebrand, McLeod & Nelson (510) 451-6732, appearing for plaintiff/moving party Karim Isaac
Erin Eileen Fry, Esq. of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP (510) 433-2600, appearing for defendant/opposing party National Railroad Passenger Corp. aka Amtrak

RULING:
PLAINTIFF KARIM ISAAC's Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement and Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Documents. ARGUED, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- PLAINTIFF KARIM ISAAC's Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement IS DENIED. C C P SECTION 664.6 REQUIRES THAT AN AGREEMENT BE SIGNED BY THE PARTIES PERSONALLY IN ORDER TO OBTAIN ENFORCEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 664.6. AN ATTORNEY'S SIGNATURE ON BEHALF OF A CLIENT IS INSUFFICIENT FOR THIS REMEDY. LEVY VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 10 CAL. 4TH 578. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Documents is denied, matter to be renoticed in Discovery Department. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =301/PJB/X-MW
